title: Pithecellobium pallens
taxonomy: Pithecellobium pallens is a species of legume in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Ingeae. The genus Pithecellobium is a part of the subtribe Robinieae within the tribe Ingeae. Pithecellobium pallens is native to Central and South America, where it grows in tropical and subtropical forests. This species is also known as the 'Yellow Acacia' due to its yellow flowers.
uses: Pithecellobium pallens has been reported to have various medicinal properties. It is traditionally used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In some regions, the plant is used to make a paste that is applied to wounds to promote healing. Additionally, Pithecellobium pallens has been used to treat digestive problems and as an antiseptic. Further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential applications.

The health benefits of Pithecellobium pallens include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as infections, wounds, and respiratory issues.
